title: Kate Starr -Hiring Manager Screen 2 | Managing Editor
meeting_title: Kate Starr -Hiring Manager Screen 2 | Managing Editor

transcript.text: Hi, Dave.
How are you?
Good.
Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you.
So you're interviewing for the managing editor role?
Yes, I am.
Okay, cool.
I'm on new internet, so we moved recently.
So let me know if the internet is choppy or if the video is choppy.
Okay, it's good now.
All right, cool.
Awesome.
So tell me about yourself.
A little bit about yourself.
What do like to do for fun?
Tell me a little bit about your journey to GrowthX and tell me all the fun things.
Yeah, okay, sure.
At the moment, I work with the SEMrush team writing content for Backlinko.
And my journey as a freelancer and...
My content marketer started right after I graduated from the university.
It's been like almost 10 years now.
And I've had a little bit of an interesting journey because I moved from digital marketing to content writing for MarTech brands.
So that was really like the experience that I had I could use in my writing as well, because I understood the audience really well.
And like, it was like I was writing for myself.
I actually learned a lot from Backlinko blog.
It was like quite amazing to then pivot into writing for the blog.
And yeah, I have been working as a digital marketing manager for a few years.
And I've been managing small teams.
I had a project with my partner.
With my business partner, we had a lead generation agency, and we built it from scratch.
So I was the one responsible for the marketing part, and I hired the developer to build our platform and website, and I was managing a team of designers, web developers, and writers.
So, yeah, during COVID, we had to unfortunately close the project, and then I pivoted into content writing, so that's what I'm doing since then, full time.
But I'm also, like, recently I also started consulting small businesses and entrepreneurs about...
And marketing.
And yeah, I also started my own content for LinkedIn.
And I started a newsletter.
So yeah.
And yeah, about five parts.
I like sports.
ski.
And I and yeah, I'm trying to be as active as possible throughout the year.
And I like traveling.
Amazing.

Yeah, that's you're like a full stack content, like you pretty much have, you know, all the experience and content from, you know, ideation, creation, all the way to from PR, you know, it looks like an outreach.
So I've actually worked with a few of these companies that you've worked with before.
So Clear Voice, I've worked with them when I was at Red Ventures.
So we always cut a bit content.
And then when you're at Codeless, I worked with them a whole bunch with a few other like consulting gigs that I had back in the day.
So
It's cool that you could have written some content for me back then.
Yeah, maybe possibly.
That's amazing.
Cool.
So it says you speak a lot of different languages here.
So I guess you grew up multilingual, but native English and Spanish, I guess.
No, not Spanish.
I grew up in Georgia.
So my first language is Georgian, but I started learning English in school.
And I understand a little bit of Russia because it's the neighboring country and a lot of people speak it here.
And recently I moved to, yeah, also in school I've been learning German because I was going to the German school.
And recently I moved to Sweden, so I'm learning Swedish at the moment.
Are you comfortable editing those languages?
No.
And say we have some clients that have content in different languages, so like they'll be Spanish or, you know, maybe it'll be German or Russian.
Are you comfortable in like reading, reviewing, and editing like in foreign languages?
Honestly, I have never had experience working in those languages.
But, like, my German was at the same level as my English, but I haven't had experience lately, so, but I would probably need to renew my knowledge and, yeah.
And then, so you were, like, writing in-house for Backlink Co, right?
basically, was there any time where, like, you saw that there was a topic or maybe that there was...

Is something that you thought it was a really great opportunity or a really good topic to go after, but you got like mixed results or you got basically when you pitched it, they weren't really like super happy about it or they were like, eh, you know, let's, let's not, you know, when there's opportunity, how do you, how do you think about, or how do you position yourself in a way where if the client or if you're, you know, leadership is not on board, like how would you pitch it to them and say like, hey, you know, I think we're, you know, you can go from there.
Like how, how would you convince them to, that that's a great opportunity and how, and how would you try and convince them that you should go after this topic?
Yeah.
So, um, in Backlinko, we actually don't, uh, pitch the topics because they, like, they have a team who, um, adds the topics in the, in our backlog.
And then we as writers have the freedom to choose which topics are interesting for us.
So I don't have that pitching experience.
But like my recent article, about my recent article, I was writing about, it was quite a complex topic.
It was about SEO forecasting and not something that many people know or like there's not a lot of information on how to do it exactly either.
And I wanted this article to be very in-depth and straightforward.
So when someone read this article, I wanted them to understand and have the actionable steps, step-by-step, how to do it themselves.
And I found it a little bit challenging to gather all this information, because it was quite technical to do it myself.
And
So there's one way that includes statistical analysis, and that's not something quite easy to understand for a lot of people.
And I tried to make a spreadsheet, but yeah, unfortunately, I have statistical background as well from the university.
But even for me, it was a little bit hard to compile that kind of resource for the readers.
So I reached out to the team, the lead editor, and I pitched him to involve someone to make this for us, and we went for that.
All right, let's walk through it.
So say you bring on a new client, and...
To Zoo, can you walk me through what your process is of how you're going to create a content strategy for them and what are the steps that you would do?
Sure.
In the beginning, it would be helpful to meet the client and understand where they already stand in content marketing.
Do they have, like, what kind of presence do they have?

And then work from there and ask questions about their audience and what they see that their audience is really interested when they visit the zoo, what it is that they ask frequently and then create the content strategy based off of that.
And add to the research, of course.
Cool.
And then...
So how would you gauge the opportunity for different pillars?
So say there was a pillar around, you've identified like four or five different pillars that you can go after.
So like lions, tigers, bears, for example.
How do you prioritize which one to go for first?
Probably based on the data, what is more relevant and which pillars can bring more traffic to the website, which are like, which have the most traffic potential.
And do you feel that when you're writing content pillars, do you feel that you should fill out of like write all the content for one pillar first?
Or should you spread that content out to multiple pillars and do some for lions, some for tigers, some for bears?
Or would you just do all?
And content first.
This is fun.
Yeah, this is fun.

I think it would be, like, depends on the, like, how big the pillar is as well, I guess, because if there's, like, 50 topics in one pillar and we focus on that, it can be, like, repetitive and we are not going to cover as much, like, we are kind of limited in that opportunity, but I'm more for covering one pillar first and then moving to the other.
Cool.
All right, I want to make sure I give you time to ask me questions.
Let's see here.
So why growthx?
Yeah, like I've been involved in AI a lot.
And when I heard about this company, I heard it from Branco.
And when I heard about this company, I was quite excited about what it does and also the role.
I think that it will be very exciting for me.
And are you currently using any like AI now in your content writing process?
Yeah, I currently use the combination of GPT and Claude, but I use in very like kind of creative ways.
I don't use it to write, write.
I use it for research, audience research.
I use it as a help to...
Give me a feedback after I write my article to make sure it aligns with our standards.
Okay.
So is there anything else that you're experimenting with AI now?
Yeah, I'm actually trying to build a process on AI now that will help me with the editing process.
And also for my own personal project, I'm trying to build a process that will make it easier to produce content from ideation to finished product.
Okay.
And then you mentioned you have some projects on the side and you're consulting.
Would you be doing those at the same time?
So if you were to get a job at GrowthX, would you also be consulting on the side and having extra work there?
Or is that...
Yeah.
I didn't really mention like what I do or any other stuff here.
So I'm a managing director.
So basically I am the director above the managing editors and I am here to like unblock roadblocks.
I'm here to help you succeed and really be your coach across the board.
So that's pretty much what I do over.
I think through like long term strategy, I think through like, you know, what we're doing six months to a year from now, like that's typically what my role is, more.
Managing editors are the ones that are really steering the ship.
They're the ones that are coming up with the content strategy.

Obviously, I'll have some input there on, you know, what I think might be good opportunities, but the managing editors are really, you know, running the ship and really creating their own content strategies and running across, running it through the calendar and making sure that it's all getting set up.
They're also having, they also have the client facing meetings.
So you will be meeting clients and talking to them.

And really, you know, being, I'll be in some of those meetings, don't have to be in all of them, but like I'll be in some of those meetings to help, you know, facilitate conversation or like we have a new technology that's coming out, you know, to pitch the client or whatever.

But you would basically, as a managing editor, be responsible for the, you know, keeping in contact, constant communication, really making sure that the client is happy and that we're, you know, communicating the strategy and success and failures just to make sure like, hey, this didn't work out right, but we're, you know, shifting priorities and doing here.
You know, we believe in like async communication, so the client can't meet that particular week.
We usually meet weekly with clients.
We'll record a loom and we'll just send them a loom and, you know, Codeless was really good at that.
So like, you know, every time I wasn't able to meet, you know, those guys would send me a loom and, you know, felt like, you know, cool things are happening.
And, you know, I was very comfortable with that.
And that's what
What we're trying to do here is really trying to make sure that our clients are comfortable and that they see all the hard work that's happening behind the scenes.
To answer your question, the opportunity really is, yes, there's opportunity to grow.
We are a new startup.
I started in March.
March 10th was my first day with GrowthX.
I'm still getting acclimated, to be honest.
There's technology moves really fast here, and we're building new flows.
We're building new processes.
We're just building the airplane as we're flying it, so it's super fun.

If you're a builder and you enjoy building cool things and you enjoy unlocking, working independently and unlocking new ways to do things and unlocking growth and being able to be your own, being entrepreneurial and being able to work on your own independently and come up with your own things.
This is cool.
This is
Because you have the freedom to really kind of do those.
if like, hey, Dave, I need so-and-so, it's like a $25 a month subscription, go get it.
Like if it's going to help you drive more business, I will help you get that.

And, you know, so it's really, in my opinion, like this place is really great because it encourages like builders and folks that are entrepreneurial about it, like folks that want to own their business.
As you continue, like you'll get a couple of clients.
It takes a little while to learn the flows.
So like, you know, I'd say like two weeks to really kind of understand the flows, to start writing your own content, editing your content.
But, you know, we had recently had someone start and it took her about two weeks to really kind of get the flow.
And then after that, it's like just getting used to launching clients, meetings, things like that.
So probably about 30 days, I would expect folks to be acclimated to the process.
Like, it's continual growth, continual learning.
Awesome.
Yeah.
It really seems exciting to be there because, yeah, all the things you've mentioned, like, if you like building and growing things for yourself, yeah, this is a really good place.
And, like, I see you moved from TripAdvisor, right?
Yes.
Yeah.
How was it to move from a big company like that to a startup?
Yeah.
So TripAdvisor is a big company, not without their pain points.
So the bigger you are, the more politics, the more, you know, there's a team working on every part of the site.
So for me to be innovative and me to drive incremental growth, it takes a lot of effort and a lot of coordination.
You're working with four or five different teams to make a small change.
And that's just too much because you move too slowly and you don't get the growth that you're looking for if you can't move fast.
And that's the problem.
So I was there for about two years.
I enjoyed it, but I like moving quick and I like being able to test and fail quickly until I find something that works.
And in that situation, you know, you can't move quickly.
And I think that's going to be an issue for them in the future.
But yeah, any time that you lose the ability to be agile, I think you start kind of losing business and that kind of puts another nail in the coffin, so to speak.
Like, yeah, yeah, cool.
So do you have any, anything else you want to know about the role or anything about growthx?
No.
I think I have quite a lot of information at this point.
Yeah, one question, like, would you like me to give any more context about what Marilia or Mariana told you about me, probably?
So I haven't been able to sync up.
So I just moved to a new house and I've been busy all weekend, like moving and trying to get acclimated to this place.
I haven't had a chance.
Bronco told me a little bit about that you have worked together in the past, and I thought that was really cool at, was it at SEMrush?
Yeah, it was in SEMrush, and also he had an agency.
We met there first, and then he referred me to Codeless as well.
I was already working there, but still.
And then we moved to SEMrush.
Did you know Jeremy Moser and all those guys?
At Codeless?
At Codeless, yeah.
No, not really.
Who was the other guy that I work with?
can't remember his name.
He was, it wasn't Jeremy, was somebody else.
don't know, another time.
But, yeah, why don't you tell me a little bit of stuff that you did with Bronco?
I think he said he had his own little agency on the side that he was working on.
What kind of stuff did you do with him?
And maybe kind of lean into some of the stuff that you did.
Like, any success metrics or anything that you had, like big wins that you had at the Backlink Co?
Yeah, at Bronco's company, I joined as a technical writer.
I was writing for Martek Brands, and and they...
Topics were, like, quite complex as well there, and, yeah, like, I did not have direct contact with the clients there, but I know that my articles drove, like, quite a good amount of traffic.

And, yeah, even in Backlinko, I have actually been, like, I've been named as someone who has, whose article got a lot of, like, most SEMrush subscriptions, and I've also been, like, I've also gotten shoutouts for getting in the top three.
Okay.

And one really exciting thing that happened while I was in Backlinko is that since I moved to Sweden, I met a lot of entrepreneurs in person and then one of them actually shared that they found my article on Backlinko organically and they were really inspired by that article.
It was about inbound marketing and they were just getting started and that article actually made them quite a good of like actionable steps.
And yeah, they got inspired and they got started based off of that article and then they also told me sometimes how to get how to proceed.
And yeah, I was happy to hear it from a reader's perspective.
It was really important.
Yeah.
It's awesome.
Well, that you're able to get, you know, that feedback from somebody who's read your piece before.
Yeah.
Really awesome.
What are some of the things that you like to write?
Like, if you had, like, you know, open space of, like, hey, you know, my dream client would be this.
Like, what would be your dream client that you could write content for?
A specific client or?
Anything.
Anything.
Yeah.
So what would be your dream client or dream topic to see if you were to, like, you know, come on board and say, you know, I really love fashion.
Like, you know, I would love to write content about fashion.
Like, that would be, like, you know, tell me about that.
Like, what you.
Yeah.
Yeah, obviously, based on my experience, I enjoy writing about MarTech and marketing because, like, I have, I already have.
have a lot of experience in it, so, like, it flows naturally, but also, like, I would.
And like I would actually be interested in writing about travel because I travel a lot and like I could share my experience, like I could use my experience in that as well.
And also the skincare niche would be interesting.
Yeah, I love travel also.
So that's one of my passions is that I love to, you know, experience different cultures and go to different places.
So I'm usually, you know, try to plan one big vacation a year or, you know, somewhere else.
That's cool.
Awesome.
Not so much for skincare.
So I'm sure, you know, there's probably a client out there that would need content for skincare.
There's probably the healthcare and beauty market is probably huge and billions of dollars.
So, yeah.
Cool.
So we have three minutes.
Is there like, you know, hopefully I've answered a bunch of your questions.
Is there any, you know, pressing anything that you need to know or any information that I haven't been able to give you yet?
No, I don't have any questions.
What about you?
I'm good.
I will.
Who is your hiring manager?
Or who is your.
Is it Marilia?
Yeah, it's Marilia who's in contact with me.
Yeah, so I will get this call.
I will talk to Marilia and figure out what next steps are and she'll get in touch.
Yeah.
Cool.
So it was really awesome to meet you and, you know, wish you luck in the next steps in the future.
Appreciate it.
Thank very much.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Hiring manager screen for Managing Editor role at GrowthX with candidate Kate Starr.](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Kate has extensive experience in content marketing, SEO, and team management, with a diverse background including work for Backlinko and SEMrush.](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)
  - [GrowthX is a fast-paced startup environment, seeking entrepreneurial individuals who can work independently and drive growth.](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
  - [The Managing Editor role involves client communication, content strategy development, and overseeing content production processes.](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Kate's multilingual background and experience with AI tools could be valuable assets for the role.](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)

## Topics

### Candidate Background

  - [10 years of experience in digital marketing and content writing](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=60.0)
  - [Worked with SEMrush team writing for Backlinko](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Experience as digital marketing manager, managing small teams](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=112.0)
  - [Co-founded a lead generation agency (closed during COVID)](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=151.0)
  - [Currently consulting small businesses on marketing](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=167.0)
  - [Started own LinkedIn content and newsletter](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=181.0)
  - [Multilingual: Georgian (native), English, some Russian, German, learning Swedish](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)

### AI and Content Creation

  - [Kate uses GPT and Claude for research and feedback, not direct writing](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=771.0)
  - [Experimenting with AI for editing process and content production workflow](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
  - [Dave emphasized GrowthX's focus on innovative AI applications in content creation](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)

### GrowthX Role and Company Culture

  - [Managing Editor position involves:](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
      - [Developing content strategies](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
      - [Client-facing meetings and communication](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=960.0)
      - [Overseeing content calendar and production](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [Company values:](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
      - [Async communication (e.g., using Loom for client updates)](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1003.0)
      - [Entrepreneurial spirit and independent work](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
      - [Fast-paced, "building the airplane as we're flying it" environment](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)
  - [Onboarding process: \~2 weeks to learn flows, \~30 days for full acclimation](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1134.0)

### Content Strategy Approach

  - [Kate's process:](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=553.0)
      - [Meet with client to understand current content marketing status](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
      - [Research audience interests and frequently asked questions](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=567.0)
      - [Prioritize content pillars based on traffic potential](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=625.0)
  - [Preference for covering one content pillar thoroughly before moving to others](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)

### Writing Preferences and Achievements

  - [Enjoys writing about MarTech and marketing due to extensive experience](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)
  - [Interest in travel and skincare niches](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [Received recognition at Backlinko for driving SEMrush subscriptions](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Positive feedback from readers on actionable content (e.g., inbound marketing article)](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1445.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Dave to discuss with Marilia (hiring manager) about next steps in the hiring process](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1646.0)
  - [Marilia will contact Kate with further information on the application process](https://fathom.video/share/bQz-iRbFEnPZJVJr1BUkAqzjpQVu-TVD?tab=summary&timestamp=1650.0)

action_items.description: Sync w/ Marilia re Kate's interview, discuss next steps
